content.text:
<analysis>
a. Event type: PR review comment - User is requesting me to investigate why MONDO IDs are appearing in a phenotype enum cache file, which seems incorrect.

b. This is primarily an analysis/investigation request, not an implementation request. I need to:
   - Analyze the problematic cache file
   - Understand why MONDO terms are in phenotype cache
   - Figure out why QC isn't catching this
   - Create a GitHub issue to track this problem

c. Key information:
   - File: cache/enums/phenotypeterm_939f99dde0f5.csv
   - Problem: MONDO disease IDs appearing in phenotype term cache
   - User concerned about QC not catching this
   - This is part of PR #814 fixing caches and term references

d. Main tasks:
   - Read and analyze the problematic cache file
   - Understand the schema and how phenotype terms should work
   - Check QC processes to see why this wasn't caught
   - Create a detailed issue describing the problem

e. Plan of action:
   - Update comment with todo list
   - Read the problematic cache file to see the MONDO entries
   - Check dismech schema to understand phenotype term constraints
   - Look at QC/validation processes
   - Investigate how these MONDO terms got into phenotype cache
   - Create GitHub issue with detailed analysis

f. I should be able to complete this analysis with the available tools (Read, Grep, etc.) and create an issue description. No special permissions needed for investigation.
</analysis>

I'll analyze the phenotype cache file issue and create a GitHub issue to track this problem.

input.body: Claude Code is working… ### Task: Investigate MONDO IDs in phenotype cache **Todo list:** - [ ] Analyze the problematic cache file to identify MONDO entries - [ ] Check dismech schema for phenotype term constraints - [ ] Investigate how MONDO terms could end up in phenotype cache - [ ] Check QC processes to understand why this wasn't caught - [ ] Create GitHub issue with detailed analysis --- [View job run](https://github.com/monarch-initiative/dismech/actions/runs/23819325504)
